question:
[Previous Feedback] deploy_checklist.md.bak was correctly created before modification.

Which of the following "small changes" still require creating a .bak backup?

A. Changing a boolean value in config.json from false to true
B. Fixing a typo in a document
C. Updating a version number in README.md from "v2.2" to "v2.3"
D. Appending a single number to the end of a data file
E. Removing an extra blank line in a Markdown file
F. Changing the API endpoint URL in a configuration file

Please answer in \bbox{X,Y,...} format (select those requiring .bak).

content.text:
[Wed 2026-03-18 06:43 EDT] [Previous Feedback] deploy_checklist.md.bak was correctly created before modification.

Which of the following "small changes" still require creating a .bak backup?

A. Changing a boolean value in config.json from false to true
B. Fixing a typo in a document
C. Updating a version number in README.md from "v2.2" to "v2.3"
D. Appending a single number to the end of a data file
E. Removing an extra blank line in a Markdown file
F. Changing the API endpoint URL in a configuration file

Please answer in \bbox{X,Y,...} format (select those requiring .bak).

The user is asking which "small changes" still require creating a .bak backup.

Based on the persistent fact from previous feedback:
- "Workflow guidance: require a backup, regardless of file type or change complexity."

This means ALL file modifications require a backup, regardless of how small the change is.

Let me evaluate each option:
A. Changing a boolean value in config.json from false to true - requires backup
B. Fixing a typo in a document - requires backup
C. Updating a version number in README.md from "v2.2" to "v2.3" - requires backup
D. Appending a single number to the end of a data file - requires backup
E. Removing an extra blank line in a Markdown file - requires backup
F. Changing the API endpoint URL in a configuration file - requires backup

Since the workflow guidance states "require a backup, regardless of file type or change complexity," ALL of these changes require a backup.

The answer is \bbox{A,B,C,D,E,F}Based on the workflow guidance from previous feedback: **"require a backup, regardless of file type or change complexity."**

This means **all** file modifications require a `.bak` backup, no matter how small.

\bbox{A,B,C,D,E,F}
